data.title: Samsonite Seeks to Add Perfetti Van Melle CEO Sameer Suneja as Independent Director
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Samsonite International SA ( (HK:1910) ) has shared an announcement.

Samsonite Group S.A. plans to seek shareholder approval at its June 4, 2026 annual general meeting to appoint Sameer Suneja, Group CEO of confectionery giant Perfetti Van Melle Group B.V., as an independent non-executive director for a term running to the 2029 annual meeting. The proposed appointment, with annual remuneration of at least US$200,000 and confirmed compliance with Hong Kong independence criteria, would add a seasoned global consumer-goods executive to Samsonite’s board, potentially strengthening its governance depth and strategic consumer-market expertise.

Suneja brings nearly three decades of leadership and brand-building experience across multinational consumer businesses, including senior roles at Perfetti Van Melle in Europe and India and earlier positions with Frito-Lay India and Colgate-Palmolive (India) Ltd. Samsonite’s nomination and remuneration committees have endorsed his independence and compensation package, and a circular detailing the proposal and voting arrangements will be sent to shareholders ahead of the meeting.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1910) stock is a Buy with a HK$26.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Samsonite International SA stock, see the HK:1910 Stock Forecast page.

More about Samsonite International SA

Samsonite Group S.A. is a Luxembourg-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the global travel and lifestyle products industry, best known for its luggage and travel bags. The group targets international consumers through a portfolio of travel-related brands and relies on a broad geographic footprint and multi-channel distribution to support its market position.

data.title: Haier Smart Home Launches 2026 Plan to Boost Value, Global Growth and Efficiency
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Haier Smart Home Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6690) ) has issued an update.

Haier Smart Home has unveiled its 2026 Corporate Value and Return Enhancement Action Plan, aiming to evolve from a traditional home appliance leader into a user-centric, platform-based technology ecosystem enterprise. The company is building a framework of one smart home channel, two customer and user experience platforms, and six core capabilities, including globalization, premiumization, digitalization, intelligentization, ecosystem integration, and greening.

On the revenue side, Haier plans to reinforce its leadership in white goods at home while targeting rapid growth in emerging overseas markets such as India, Southeast Asia, the Middle East, Africa, and Brazil. It is also accelerating the integration and expansion of its large HVAC business and cultivating emerging areas like smart home solutions, small appliances, healthcare and elderly care services, and recycling to diversify revenue and drive long-term growth.

On the efficiency side, the company is upgrading its operating model in China through a unified warehousing ToC system and an optimized logistics network to improve inventory turnover and terminal service capabilities. Overseas, Haier is focusing on brand building and upgrading to enhance operational efficiency and better capture global growth opportunities, supporting sustainable, high-quality development and improved investor returns.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6690) stock is a Hold with a HK$23.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Haier Smart Home Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:6690 Stock Forecast page.

More about Haier Smart Home Co., Ltd. Class H

Haier Smart Home Co., Ltd. is a China-based global home appliance manufacturer focused on white goods, HVAC systems, and smart home solutions. The company targets both domestic and overseas markets, leveraging strengths in manufacturing, digitalization, and green, intelligent products to serve evolving consumer demand and expand its international footprint.

data.description:

The latest announcement is out from YesAsia Holdings Limited ( (HK:2209) ).

YesAsia Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for 18 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited financial statements and directors’ and auditors’ reports for the year ended 31 December 2025. The agenda also includes re-election of directors, approval of their remuneration, re-appointment of RSM Hong Kong as auditors, and the declaration of a final dividend of HK$0.10 per share for shareholders on record as of 2 July 2026.

Shareholders will further vote on granting the board a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, excluding any treasury shares, including through rights issues and other share-based arrangements. The proposed mandate, which would remain in force until the next annual meeting or earlier revocation, is designed to give the board flexibility for potential capital raising and corporate actions, potentially influencing YesAsia’s funding options and strategic positioning in the market.

More about YesAsia Holdings Limited

YesAsia Holdings Limited, incorporated in Hong Kong and listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong, operates in the retail and e-commerce sector, focusing on Asian media, entertainment, and lifestyle products for global consumers. The company targets both local and international markets through online platforms and associated services, leveraging its Hong Kong base as a regional hub.

Guan Chao Holdings Limited ( (HK:1872) ) has issued an update.

Guan Chao Holdings Limited, a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, is overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors and is audited by ZHONGHUI ANDA CPA Limited under the Hong Kong Listing Rules. The company has called its annual general meeting for 22 May 2026 in Hong Kong to adopt the 2025 audited accounts, re-elect four directors, approve directors’ and auditor’s remuneration and grant a general mandate for the board to issue additional shares within specified limits, which could provide financial flexibility and influence capital structure and shareholder dilution.

The AGM will ask shareholders to authorise the directors to allot and issue new shares up to 20% of the existing share capital, with a further uplift tied to any shares repurchased by the company within a defined period. This proposed general mandate, together with the routine re-appointment of the auditor and setting of board pay, reflects standard Hong Kong corporate practice while signaling the company’s intention to maintain access to equity financing options and confirm its current governance framework.

More about Guan Chao Holdings Limited

Guan Chao Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates as a publicly traded company whose shares are traded on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. Its governance structure includes executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, and it is subject to the Listing Rules in Hong Kong.

Zhengye International Holdings has agreed to dispose of an industrial property in Zhongshan, Guangdong, through its indirect non-wholly owned subsidiary Zhongshan Lianhe Hongxing Zhizao Company Limited. The latest announcement is out from Jiangsu Guofu Hydrogen Energy Equipment Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2582) ).

Jiangsu Guofu Hydrogen Energy Equipment Co., Ltd. has announced that its Hong Kong subsidiary, Guofu Hydrogen Energy HK, has entered into a subscription agreement to acquire newly issued class A ordinary shares of an independent target company for about US$3.6 million. Upon completion, Guofu Hydrogen Energy HK will hold roughly 8.70% of the target’s class A ordinary shares, representing about 8.50% of its enlarged issued share capital.

The move is classified as a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, as the applicable percentage ratio exceeds 5% but is below 25%, triggering notification and announcement requirements. Completion remains subject to closing conditions in the subscription agreement, meaning investors are cautioned that the transaction may not proceed as expected and should seek professional advice when dealing in the company’s H shares.

data.title: Huscoke Disputes HK$700,000 Statutory Demand Amid Harassment Claims and Ongoing Trading Suspension
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Huscoke Holdings Limited ( (HK:0704) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Huscoke Holdings Limited has received a statutory demand from Anpa Financial Services Group Limited seeking payment of about HK$700,000 for financial printing and related services provided between 2022 and 2023, a claim the company disputes amid allegations of harassment and intimidation by a person claiming to represent the creditor. The board believes the disputed amount is not material to its operations, questions the validity of the demand, plans to lodge objections and negotiate within the 21-day deadline, and notes that its shares remain suspended from trading on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange while advising investors to exercise caution.

The company has reported to police several incidents in early 2024 involving nuisance letters, threatening calls, emails, and alleged improper collection of personal data targeting a director and the company secretary, which it considers to potentially constitute criminal offences beyond a normal commercial fee dispute. While the board maintains that the situation does not amount to inside information requiring mandatory disclosure, it issued this voluntary announcement at the request of the Stock Exchange’s Listing Division and will provide further updates in line with statutory requirements.

More about Huscoke Holdings Limited

Huscoke Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company, though the announcement focuses on its capital market status rather than detailing its core business activities or products. The company’s shares are currently suspended from trading on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, and it is managing ongoing regulatory and legal communications in that context.

Average Trading Volume: 482,488

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$115M

Guangdong – Hong Kong Greater Bay Area Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1396) ) just unveiled an update.

Guangdong – Hong Kong Greater Bay Area Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for 17 June 2026 in Shenzhen, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited financial statements and director and auditor reports for the year ended 31 December 2025. The AGM will also ask shareholders to re-elect two executive directors and three independent non-executive directors, authorise the board to set their remuneration, re-appoint Prism Hong Kong Limited as the company’s auditor for 2026, and grant the board a general mandate to issue additional shares, including potential treasury share transactions, within defined limits.

Giordano International ( (HK:0709) ) has shared an announcement.

Giordano International has named non-executive director Sonia Cheng as chairman of the board, while Patrick Tsang steps down from the chairman role but remains on the nomination committee. Cheng, a prominent Hong Kong business leader and CEO of Rosewood Hotel Group, will also chair the nomination committee and sit on the remuneration committee, reflecting an effort to leverage her extensive corporate and public-service experience.

The company has also reshaped its board structure by redesignating executive director Jacob Lee as a non-executive director and removing him from the management committee. Independent directors Victor Huang and Kelvin Chau have been added to the remuneration and nomination committees respectively, signaling a broader refresh of governance and oversight frameworks as Giordano adjusts its leadership mix and committee composition.

More about Giordano International

Giordano International is a Hong Kong-listed apparel retailer known for its casual wear and accessories. China Mengniu Dairy Co ( (HK:2319) ) has issued an update.

China Mengniu Dairy Company Limited has called its annual general meeting for 5 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements and directors’ and auditors’ reports for the year ended 31 December 2025. The board will also seek approval for a proposed final dividend of RMB0.52 per share, re-election of three directors, and reappointment of KPMG as auditor with authority to set its remuneration.

Shareholders will additionally vote on an ordinary resolution granting the board a general mandate to repurchase up to 10% of the company’s issued share capital, excluding treasury shares, on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange or other recognised exchanges. This share buyback authority, if granted, would give Mengniu greater flexibility in managing its capital structure and could influence share liquidity and returns to investors over the coming year.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2319) stock is a Buy with a HK$21.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Mengniu Dairy Co stock, see the HK:2319 Stock Forecast page.

China Mengniu Dairy Company Limited is a leading dairy producer based in China, listed in Hong Kong and incorporated in the Cayman Islands.
